content.full_text:
SMUD
SACRAMENTO MUNICIPAL UTILITY DISTRICT □ P. O. Box 15830, Sacramento CA 95852-1830, (916) 452-3211
AN ELECTRIC SYSTEM SERVING THE HEART OF CALIFORNIA

LEC 92-640

October 30, 1992

Jeanette Turvill, Esq.
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Advice Letter - Financial Interest In Parent Company

Dear Jeanette:

Per our conversation of October 27, I am requesting an
advice letter based on the following facts:

The Sacramento Municipal Utility District ("District") is
planning to construct a cogeneration power plant which will also
supply steam to an ice production facility and the Sacramento
County Wastewater Treatment Plant (the "Project"). The District,
with the County of Sacramento has formed the Central Valley
Financing Authority, a joint powers authority (JPA). The JPA will
provide financing for the Project.

The developer ("Carson"), pursuant to its contract with
the District has formed a California General Partnership with an
Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) contractor, Century
Contractors West, Inc. ("Century"). The parties to the EPC
contract are the JPA and the partnership (Carson/Century).

Century Contractors West, Inc. is a wholly owned
subsidiary of Midwest Capital Group, Inc. (MCG) which, in turn, is
wholly owned by Midwest Resources Inc. (MWR) of Des Moines Iowa.
Midwest Resources Inc. was formed upon the merger of Iowa Public
Service Company and Iowa Power, two utility operating
subsidiaries. MWR's other diversified businesses, including
Century, are operated through its MCG subsidiary.

The District's Chief Financial Officer (CFO), a
designated employee, owned stock in Iowa Public Service Co. That
investment became stock in MWR after the merger. The stock of MWR
is registered with the New York Stock Exchange. As of February 28,
1992, the aggregate market value of MWR voting stock held by non-
affiliates was $1,055,044,475 when a total of 53,870,803 shares of
common stock without par value, were outstanding. The CFO
currently owns 247 shares of MWR stock with a market value of
$4,176.

DISTRICT HEADQUARTERS □ 6201 S Street, Sacramento CA 95817-1899

Jeanette Turville, Esq.                -2-                October 30, 1992


         The CFO was not involved with the negotiation or approval
of the EPC contract to which Century is signatory as a general
partner, nor will CFO be involved in any aspect of the EPC contract
administration or control or be in a position to influence payment
to Century under that contract.  The EPC contract, however, will
most likely result in more than $250,000 gross revenues to Century.

         The CFO, as an officer of the JPA, will be involved in
the issuance of bonds to fund the Project and in procuring a bank
letter of credit which would guarantee the bonds issued in the name
of the JPA.  The financing and guarantee will pay Project costs as
well as, among other things, Century's profit.  Century will not be
a party to, or otherwise involved with, the bank or bond rating
agencies in negotiations to secure the letter of credit and
funding, except to provide information to those parties as
required.  Furthermore, even though financing for the project is
obtained through the efforts of the CFO, he will not in any way be
involved with the decisions to distribute or not distribute funds
to Century or the partnership for performance or nonperformance
under the EPC contract.

                              QUESTIONS

         Does CFO have a direct or indirect financial interest in
Century by virtue of his ownership of 247 shares of stock worth
$4,176, in Century's parent corporation, MWR, which would require
him to disclose the relationship and/or otherwise be disqualified
from securing financing for the Project and making provisions for
guaranteeing the debt?

         Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.  If
I can provide you with any additional information please call me at
732-6126.


                                          Sincerely,

                                          [signature]
                                          Dana S. Appling
                                          Senior Attorney

sections.question:
Does CFO have a direct or indirect financial interest in Century by virtue of his ownership of 247 shares of stock worth $4,176, in Century's parent corporation, MWR, which would require him to disclose the relationship and/or otherwise be disqualified from securing financing for the Project and making provisions for guaranteeing the debt?

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. If I can provide you with any additional information please call me at 732-6126.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. If I can provide you with any additional information please call me at 732-6126.
